dossier.overview:
An `.rtpks` file is a **Resort Tile Pack Source** — a portable ZIP archive that stores a Pokémon DS Map Studio (PDSMS) tileset together with a stable **Resort tile ID** index. PDSMS continues to be the authoring UI for Gen 4/5-style 3D tile painting; RTPKS is how those tiles survive re-saves, merges, and handoff into the Resort toolchain without renumbering identities.

RTPKS is a **source** format (editor + asset library). Overworld **maps** for the C++ runtime are authored separately in the **Operations Desk Map Editor** as `.owmap` files (height, collision, spawn, metadata). Tile packs and terrain maps solve different problems — RTPKS holds *what tiles exist*; owmap holds *where the player walks*.

Implementation: `Pokemon-DS-Map-Studio/src/main/java/resort/formats/`. In-repo overview: `Pokemon-DS-Map-Studio/docs/pokemon_resort_pipeline.md` (RTPKS sections).

sections.title: What RTPKS is for
sections.summary: Stable tile identities for PDSMS → Resort.

blocks.body: Pokémon DS Map Studio with the **Pokémon Resort** menu open. RTPKS actions live here: **Open RTPKS as Tileset…**, **Save Current Tileset as RTPKS…**, and **Append Current Tiles to RTPKS…**. Paint maps with the normal tile list on the right — local indices in the grid, stable `resortTileId` values inside the pack file.
blocks.caption: Pokémon Resort menu — RTPKS tileset actions

blocks.title: Resort authoring split
blocks.caption: Two editors, two formats — linked by pipeline discipline, not one mega-file
blocks.source:
flowchart LR
    PDSMS[PDSMS tile painting] -->|Save as RTPKS| RTPKS[.rtpks tile pack]
    RTPKS -->|stable resortTileId| TILES[Shared tile library]
    ME[Map Editor owmap] -->|height collision spawn| OWM[.owmap terrain map]
    OWM --> GAME[C++ overworld loader]
    TILES -.->|future tile placement| ME

blocks.body:
Design goals (from the Resort PDSMS integration):

• **No second tile palette UI** — artists use the existing PDSMS tile selector; stable IDs live only inside RTPKS.
• **Native payload round-trip** — `pdsms/tileset.pdsts` loads through existing `TilesetIO`; the editor never reconstructs tiles from runtime meshes.
• **One Tile = one identity** — a 2×2 tree is still one RTPKS entry, not four draw calls.
• **ID preservation** — re-saving an RTPKS-backed tileset keeps existing `resortTileId` values; removed slots become `inactive` tombstones instead of silent renumbering.

Empty map cells in PDSMS use local tile index **-1**. Resort tile ID **0 is valid** — do not treat zero as “null id.”

sections.title: Archive schema
sections.summary: ZIP layout, manifest, tile index, and PDSMS payload.

blocks.body: Extension: `.rtpks`. Container: **ZIP archive** with forward-slash entry paths (no custom binary header beyond ZIP). Reader/writer: `RtpksArchive.java` — shared ZIP helper also used by other Resort container formats in PDSMS.
Required entries: `manifest.json`, `index/tile_index.json`, `pdsms/tileset.pdsts`, and every PNG referenced by the tileset under `pdsms/textures/`. Thumbnail and metadata paths are optional.
blocks.caption: RTPKS archive tree

blocks.html:
Entry pathRequiredContents
manifest.jsonyesFormat id, version, pack metadata, paths to payload
index/tile_index.jsonyeslocalIndex → resortTileId mapping
pdsms/tileset.pdstsyesNative PDSMS tileset binary
pdsms/textures/*.pngyes*Embedded textures (*one per material in tileset)
pdsms/TilesetThumbnail.pngnoSelector preview image
metadata/tile_metadata.jsonnoReserved for future per-tile tags

blocks.title: Load sequence
blocks.caption: RtpksReader extracts to a temp folder because TilesetIO expects textures beside pdsts
blocks.source:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Disk as .rtpks ZIP
    participant Reader as RtpksReader
    participant IO as TilesetIO
    participant UI as PDSMS editor
    Disk->>Reader: read manifest + index + blobs
    Reader->>Reader: extract pdsts + PNGs to temp/pdsms/
    Reader->>IO: readTilesetFromFile(pdsts)
    IO->>UI: Tileset + ResortTilesetBinding

blocks.html:
FieldTypeValue / notes
formatstringpokemon_resort.rtpks
versionnumber1
packIdstringStable pack slug (from filename on first save)
namestringDisplay label
tileIndexstringPath to index JSON (default index/tile_index.json)
editorPayload.typestringMust be pdsms_tileset
editorPayload.tilesetstringDefault pdsms/tileset.pdsts
editorPayload.textureDirectorystringDefault pdsms/textures
editorPayload.thumbnailstringDefault pdsms/TilesetThumbnail.png

blocks.caption: RtpksManifest — format constant and editor payload paths

blocks.body: Format: `pokemon_resort.tile_index`, version 1. Maps each **PDSMS local tile list index** to a stable **`resortTileId`** (uint32 — stored as JSON number). Runtime and future Resort importers resolve painted cells through this table.

blocks.html:
entries[] fieldTypeDescription
localIndexnumberPosition in PDSMS tile list (0-based)
resortTileIdnumberStable Resort id (0 is valid)
keystringDiagnostic slug e.g. tile_0017
statusstringactive or inactive (tombstone)

ID assignment rules (TileIndexBuilder):

  • First save: ids allocated sequentially from 0 upward for each tile slot.
  • Re-save: existing active mappings for unchanged indices are preserved.
  • Removed tiles: prior entry kept with status: inactive — ids are not recycled silently.
  • New tiles appended at end: receive the next unused id (may skip gaps from inactive entries).
  • Any future compact/reindex tool must be explicit and emit a remap report.

blocks.caption: buildOrUpdate — preserve stable ids across saves

blocks.html:
Menu actionBehavior
Open RTPKS as Tileset…Load ZIP → native tileset + textures + ResortTilesetBinding (active tile index)
Save Current Tileset as RTPKS…Write pdsts, textures, thumbnail, manifest, and updated tile index (preserves ids)
Append Current Tiles to RTPKS…Merge editor tiles onto an existing pack; new slots get new ids; reopens pack
Validate Current Resort Setup…Checks tileset is RTPKS-backed, index matches tile count, map cells resolve ids

Status bar shows active RTPKS path and dirty state. Code: ResortController, ResortTilesetWorkflow, ResortTilesetBinding.

blocks.body: After **Append Current Tiles to RTPKS…**, PDSMS reports how many tiles were merged, the new pack size, and the **local index → resortTileId** assignments for appended slots (e.g. local 390–778 in a 779-tile pack). Existing ids are preserved; only new list positions receive fresh mappings via TileIndexBuilder.
blocks.caption: Append to RTPKS — stable id assignment report

blocks.body:
Typical tile pack workflow:

1. Build or import tiles in PDSMS (ground, trees, props on layers 0–7).
2. **Pokémon Resort → Save Current Tileset as RTPKS…** → e.g. `packs/gen4_exterior.rtpks`.
3. **Open RTPKS as Tileset…** on another machine — tile count, order, sizes, and multi-cell tiles must match.
4. Paint maps with the normal PDSMS grid (local indices only in the map matrix).
5. For Resort C++ overworld terrain (height, ramps, collision, spawn), use the **Map Editor** and export `.owmap` (see owmap-format).

Run PDSMS: Java 8+, `java -jar Pokemon-DS-Map-Studio/build/libs/pokemon-ds-map-studio.jar` (or Gradle build in `Pokemon-DS-Map-Studio/`).

blocks.caption: RtpksWriter.write — assemble ZIP from live tileset
RtpksReader.load — validate manifest and hydrate TilesetIO

sections.title: Validation rules
sections.summary: What Validate Current Resort Setup checks.

blocks.html:
CheckSeverity
Tileset loadederror
Active tileset bound to RTPKS (ResortTilesetBinding)error
Tile index non-emptyerror
Active index count == tileset.size()error
No duplicate active resortTileIderror
Each painted local index resolves to a Resort iderror
tilePackSource path mismatch vs active RTPKSwarning

blocks.body:
Reader errors (load time):

• Wrong `format` or unsupported `version` in manifest or tile index
• Missing archive entries (`manifest.json`, pdsts, textures)
• `editorPayload.type` not `pdsms_tileset`
• Texture missing during `TilesetIO` hydration

sections.title: How to extend RTPKS
sections.summary: Version bumps, metadata, and Map Editor handoff.

blocks.body:
**Safe extensions (v1):**

• **`metadata/tile_metadata.json`** — optional per-tile tags (path reserved in docs; writer does not populate yet). Readers should ignore unknown ZIP entries.
• **Extra manifest fields** — preserve on read/write if you add tooling; bump docs when semantics are defined.
• **Append workflow** — `RtpksAppender` merges tilesets and runs `TileIndexBuilder` so new slots get fresh ids without rewriting old ones.

**Breaking changes:**

• Increment manifest `version` when JSON shape changes; teach `RtpksReader.validateManifest` and any Resort importer together.
• Replacing `pdsms_tileset` payload type requires a new `editorPayload.type` — do not overload v1.
• Explicit reindex/compact tools must emit a remap report if `localIndex` or `resortTileId` assignments change.

**Map Editor (.owmap) integration:** owmap stores terrain height, special ramps, collision, spawn, and JSON metadata — not PDSMS tile layers. When bridging RTPKS tiles into Resort maps, treat RTPKS as the **canonical tile asset pack** and owmap as the **walkable world shell**. Document new glue tools in both this article and owmap-format when import paths land.

blocks.title: Extension decision flow
blocks.caption: Prefer new optional ZIP entries before bumping version

items.label: owmap format (Resort Map Editor terrain maps)
